body:
## Review Summary

Good restructuring — overlapping header download with state sync is the right architecture and the wrapper/inner pattern for `download_headers_background` ensures the `headers_downloading` flag is always cleared. Three issues: a duplicate pivot hash in `block_hashes` that corrupts the `forkchoice_update` number mapping (line 151), a `current_head` / `current_head_number` mismatch risk in the background task (line 263), and a duplicate log message (line 672).

comments.body:
The pivot hash is pushed here before spawning the background download. But `download_headers_background_inner` will also push it when it reaches `sync_head` — the `process_incoming_headers` call at line 333 extends `block_hashes` with all received headers including the sync_head.

This means the pivot hash appears twice in `block_hashes`. The `forkchoice_update` at line 684 computes `pivot_header.number - i` for each entry, so the duplicate shifts all preceding block numbers by one — every header before the pivot gets mapped to the wrong block number.

Fix: either skip pushing here and let the background task handle it, or add a guard in `process_incoming_headers` / the background loop to skip the sync_head hash if it's already present.

`current_head` (hash) is re-derived here from `get_latest_canonical_block_hash()` + checkpoint, but `current_head_number` was computed by the caller from a *different* `current_head` value (the one from `block_sync_state.get_current_head()`). Between those two reads, `sync_cycle_snap` stored the pivot header via `add_block_headers`, which could change what `get_latest_canonical_block_hash()` returns.

If the re-derived `current_head` hash doesn't match `current_head_number`, the first `request_block_headers(current_head_number, sync_head)` call starts from the wrong position.

Consider passing both `current_head` and `current_head_number` from the caller instead of re-deriving the hash.

nit: This duplicates the log at line 338 inside `download_headers_background_inner`. Since `header_download_handle.await??` only succeeds when the background task completed successfully (which already logged "complete"), this second message is redundant. Consider removing one of them or changing this to a debug-level log like `debug!("Header download handle joined successfully")`.
